title: Meum mutellina
intro: Meum mutellina, also known as meum or meum herb, is a perennial plant species belonging to the carrot family (Apiaceae). Traditionally, Meum mutellina has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Meum mutellina include sesquiterpene lactones, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. Meum mutellina is typically used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, although it is not as widely used as other herbal remedies and its efficacy and safety may vary depending on individual circumstances.
taxonomy: Meum mutellina is a flowering plant in the family Apiaceae, commonly known as carrot or parsley family. It is native to Europe and Western Asia. Within its family, Meum mutellina is classified in the tribe Scandiceae and genus Meum. The plant is often found in moist and shaded areas, such as woodlands and along streams. It is a perennial herb with finely divided leaves and small white flowers.
